entries.issue: 47 row(s) form 23 exact-duplicate group(s); removing copies drops 24 row(s).
entries.recommendation: remove duplicate copies, keep one row per group
entries.rationale: Exact duplicates almost always indicate a data-load error. Keeping one representative per group preserves the unique observations.

options.label: Remove the duplicate copies
options.description: Keep one row from each duplicate group and drop the extra copies.

options.label: Keep every row
options.description: Make no change — leave the duplicate rows in place.

options.label: Delete every duplicated row
options.description: Remove all rows involved in a duplicate, including the first copy.

entries.rationale: Near-duplicate detection requires an id-like key column to define what counts as 'the same entity twice'. None was found, so only exact-duplicate detection ran. (A field-difference rule would flood with false positives on data where distinct rows legitimately share most attributes.)

options.label: Skip this check
options.description: No action needed — move on.

entries.rationale: 6% missing and pattern is MAR — mode imputation keeps the modal category share intact; consider the missing-indicator alternative if the missingness itself may be informative.
entries.recommended_label: Fill the gaps with the most common value

options.description: Leave the values blank but add a yes/no column recording which rows were missing.

entries.rationale: Column is right-skewed (skew=12.56). Quantile bins create equal-sized groups, which are robust to skew and extreme values.

options.label: Bin into quantile groups
options.description: Divide into N groups of roughly equal size (one group per quantile).

options.label: Bin into equal-width intervals
options.description: Divide the value range into N equally-spaced bins.

options.label: Bin at natural breaks
options.description: Find natural cluster boundaries in the distribution (Jenks/k-means).

options.label: Keep as continuous
options.description: Make no change — leave the column as a numeric variable.

entries.issue: range (1.44e+06) is large relative to other numeric columns
entries.rationale: Column has range (1.44e+06) is large relative to other numeric columns. Robust scaling uses the median and IQR, which are unaffected by extreme values.

options.label: Robust-scale (median / IQR)
options.description: Centre on the median and scale by the interquartile range — unaffected by extreme values.

options.label: Standardise (z-score)
options.description: Subtract the mean and divide by the standard deviation → mean 0, std 1.

options.label: Scale to [0, 1]
options.description: Shift and rescale so all values fall between 0 and 1.

options.label: Log-transform (log1p)
options.description: Apply log(1 + x) to compress a right-skewed distribution.

options.label: Keep unscaled
options.description: Make no change — leave the column on its current scale.

entries.issue: 40 unique values — too many for one-hot encoding
entries.rationale: With 40 categories, one-hot encoding would add 40 columns. Frequency encoding replaces each value with its relative frequency, capturing cardinality information in a single numeric column.

options.label: Frequency encode
options.description: Replace each category with its relative frequency in the dataset.

options.label: Leave as categorical
options.description: Make no change — keep the column as-is.
